triq Efficient Schedulability Testing for PTIDES Christos Stergiou Citation Christos Stergiou. "Efficient Schedulability Testing for PTIDES". Talk or presentation, 7, November, 2013; Presented at the 10th Biennial Ptolemy Miniconference , Berkeley.

10th Biennial Ptolemy Miniconference , Berkeley. Abstract The schedulability problem for a restricted class of Ptides systems with constant delays is reduced to checking feasibility of generalized multiframe tasks. The latter are systems of independ

ltiframe tasks. The latter are systems of independent sporadic tasks that release jobs whose execution times, deadlines, and separation times differ across invocations. A pseudo-polynomial algorithm exists for checking feasibility for such task syste

xists for checking feasibility for such task systems. Furthermore, the same reduction is shown to provide a sufficient schedulability test in the case of a richer class of Ptides systems Electronic downloads Stergiou_EfficientSchedulability_PtolemyMC
